title: Will the 7-day moving average of transit calls through the Strait of Hormuz as reported by the IMF PortWatch be above 60 before July 1, 2026?

current.lean_note: ADVERSARIAL VETO: The forecaster's 'NO' position assumes a future crisis with no present indicators, diverging sharply from the market's 7.3% implied probability. The 5-ships vs 60 threshold gap is extreme but uncorroborated by liquid market data. Overconfid
adversarial_concerns: Assumes a 2026 crisis without current evidence; market implies higher probability of recovery
Relies on speculative scenario (90% collapse) without credible sourcing
Overlooks geopolitical/economic factors that could alter transit patterns
current.rationale_summary: 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis collapsed transit >90%; IMF PortWatch 7-day MA ~5 ships vs the 60 threshold, no recovery before the window. Effectively determined NO.

history.rationale_summary: Reaching a 7-day MA of 60 (~50% of normal) by July 1 from today's ~3/day requires full physical reopening within ~18 days; mine clearing (months), war-risk insurance reinstatement, and vessel repositioning make this near-impossible even if a deal is signed this week.
Blockade entrenched; even a ceasefire leaves demining+insurance+carrier lag beyond ~18 days. Modal NO; crowd-adjusted partway up from 0.04.

history.lean_note: ADVERSARIAL VETO: The forecaster's position relies on unspecified factors and short-term observations to predict a low probability of Hormuz normalization by 2026, diverging significantly from the market. Without concrete evidence, credible sourcing, or anal
adversarial_concerns: Lack of specific, credible evidence for 'toll/diplomatic stalemate' or 'backlog' blocking normalization; vague reasoning
Overreliance on model's 'unchanged since yesterday' as a long-term predictor
Divergence from market implies unexplained edge not justified by evidence
Base-rate neglect: historical normalization trends not addressed
history.rationale_summary: Held 3-Opus ensemble: toll/diplomatic stalemate + backlog block full normalization by Jul 1; world unchanged since yesterday.
